functions.name: addHelmRelease
functions.description: Installs a Helm chart into the k3s cluster.
functions.remarks:
The release runs as an `alpine/helm` container on the DCP network. No host-side
`helm` binary is required. The container exits with code 0 when the release is
fully installed and all workloads are ready. Use `WaitForCompletion(helmRelease)`
on resources that must start only after the chart is ready.
Customize the release with `WithHelmValue` for individual key/value pairs
or `WithHelmValuesFile` to supply a full YAML values file.
functions.signature: addHelmRelease(name: string, chart: string, repo?: string, version?: string, namespace?: string): HelmReleaseResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name; also used as the Helm release name passed to `helm upgrade --install`.

parameters.name: chart
parameters.description: The chart name, e.g. `argo-cd` for a repo chart or `oci://registry/chart` for an OCI reference. Provide `repo` when using a named repo chart.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: Optional Helm repository URL, e.g. `https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm`. When provided, the repo is added and updated before installation.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Optional chart version to pin, e.g. `7.8.0`. When `null` the latest version available in the repository is installed.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: The Kubernetes namespace to install the release into. The namespace is created automatically if it does not exist. Defaults to `default`.

functions.name: addK3sCluster
functions.description: Adds a k3s Kubernetes cluster to the distributed application.
functions.remarks:
The cluster runs as a privileged container using the `rancher/k3s` image.
No host-side `kubectl`, `helm`, or `k3s` binaries are required.
Three kubeconfig variants are written to `{AppHostDirectory}/.k3s/{name}/`
when the cluster becomes ready:
-
-
Call `WithReference(cluster)` on a dependent resource builder to inject
these credentials automatically.
All other cluster options are available as fluent builder methods:
`WithK3sVersion`, `WithAgentCount`, `WithPodSubnet`,
`WithServiceSubnet`, `WithDisabledComponent`,
`WithExtraArg`, `WithDataVolume`, `WithHelmImage`,
`WithKubectlImage`, and `WithLifetime`.
functions.signature: addK3sCluster(name: string, apiServerPort?: number, agentCount?: number): K3sClusterResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The resource name. Also used as the DNS hostname by which containers in the DCP network reach the cluster's API server (e.g. `https://{name}:6443`).

parameters.name: apiServerPort
parameters.description: Host port to bind the Kubernetes API server (port 6443) to. When `null` (the default) a random available port is assigned.

parameters.name: agentCount
parameters.description: Number of k3s agent (worker) nodes to add. When `null` (the default) a single-node cluster is created — the server node acts as both control-plane and worker. Equivalent to calling `WithAgentCount` on the returned builder.

functions.name: addK8sManifest
functions.description: Applies Kubernetes YAML manifests or a Kustomize overlay to the cluster.
functions.remarks:
The apply mode is detected automatically from `path`:
-
-
-
The container exits with code 0 after all manifests are applied and any CRDs have
reached the `Established` condition. No host-side `kubectl` binary is required.
Use `WaitForCompletion(manifest)` on resources that must start only after the
manifests are applied.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name for this manifest application.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path to a single `.yaml`/`.yml` file, a directory of YAML files, or a Kustomize overlay directory (containing `kustomization.yaml`). Relative paths are resolved against the AppHost project directory.

functions.name: addServiceEndpoint
functions.description: Exposes a Kubernetes Service from the cluster as an Aspire endpoint resource.
functions.remarks:
An in-process WebSocket port-forward is started when the cluster becomes ready,
binding to `0.0.0.0:{allocatedHostPort}` so both host processes and DCP-network
containers can reach the service.
The endpoint transitions to `Running` only after the target Kubernetes Service
has at least one ready pod. Sequence chart installs or manifest applies before this
resource using `WaitForCompletion` on `HelmReleaseResource` or
`K8sManifestResource` to prevent the port-forward from polling indefinitely.
Use `WithReference(endpoint)` on a dependent resource builder to inject the
service URL as `services__{name}__url`. Host processes receive
`http(s)://localhost:{port}`; containers receive
`http(s)://host.docker.internal:{port}`.
functions.signature: addServiceEndpoint(name: string, serviceName: string, servicePort: number, namespace?: string, scheme?: string): K3sServiceEndpointResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name for this endpoint.

parameters.name: serviceName
parameters.description: The name of the Kubernetes Service to forward, as it appears in `kubectl get svc`.

parameters.name: servicePort
parameters.description: The port number declared on the Kubernetes Service (the `port` field, not `targetPort`). Must be in the range 1–65535.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: The Kubernetes namespace that contains the Service. Defaults to `default`.

parameters.name: scheme
parameters.description: The URL scheme (`http` or `https`) for the injected environment variable. When `null` (the default), the scheme is inferred from the port: ports 443 and 8443 use `https`, all others use `http`.

functions.name: namespace
functions.description: Gets the Kubernetes namespace the chart is installed into.

functions.name: parent
functions.description: Gets the Parent property

functions.name: releaseName
functions.description: Gets the Helm release name passed to `helm upgrade --install`.

functions.name: apiEndpoint
functions.description: Gets the endpoint reference for the k3s API server (port 6443).

functions.name: connectionStringEnvironmentVariable
functions.description: Overrides the default `ConnectionStrings__` prefix so Aspire injects `KUBECONFIG`.

functions.name: connectionStringExpression
functions.description: Manifest expression for the local kubeconfig path.

functions.name: connectionStringEnvironmentVariable
functions.description: The environment variable name used to inject the service URL into dependents (`services__{name}__url`), following the Aspire service-discovery convention.

functions.name: connectionStringExpression
functions.description: Gets the manifest expression for the service URL. Resolves to `http(s)://localhost:{hostPort}` when the endpoint is ready.

functions.name: hostPort
functions.description: Gets the host port bound by the port-forward listener.
functions.remarks:
Zero until the resource transitions to `Running`. Use `WaitFor(endpoint)`
to ensure this is populated before reading it from a dependent resource.

functions.name: namespace
functions.description: Gets the Kubernetes namespace that contains the Service.

functions.name: parent
functions.description: Gets the Parent property

functions.name: serviceName
functions.description: Gets the name of the Kubernetes Service being forwarded.

functions.name: servicePort
functions.description: Gets the port number declared on the Kubernetes Service.

functions.name: setHostPort
functions.description: Gets the host port bound by the port-forward listener.
functions.remarks:
Zero until the resource transitions to `Running`. Use `WaitFor(endpoint)`
to ensure this is populated before reading it from a dependent resource.

functions.name: parent
functions.description: Gets the Parent property

functions.name: path
functions.description: Gets the absolute host path to the YAML file, plain directory, or Kustomize directory that contains the manifests to apply.

functions.name: withAgentCount
functions.description: Sets the number of k3s agent (worker) nodes to add to the cluster.
functions.remarks:
Agent nodes connect to the server via DCP DNS (`https://{name}:6443`) and use
k3s's built-in retry loop, so no explicit `WaitFor` is needed. The cluster health
check waits for `1 + count` nodes to reach `Ready` state before reporting
healthy. Use `WithLifetime` with `Persistent`
to keep agents alive across AppHost restarts and avoid node password hash mismatches.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: The number of agent nodes. Zero or greater. Defaults to `0` (single-node cluster — the server node acts as both control-plane and worker).

functions.name: withDataVolume
functions.description: Mounts a named Docker volume at the k3s data directory so cluster state persists across AppHost restarts.
functions.remarks:
The volume covers `/var/lib/rancher/k3s`, which contains the SQLite database,
TLS certificates, and kubeconfig. Without this volume the cluster starts fresh on
every AppHost launch. Combine with `ContainerLifetime.Persistent` on the cluster
resource and its dependent Helm releases to avoid re-installing charts on every start.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Optional volume name. When `null` (the default) a name is generated from the application and resource names.

functions.name: withDisabledComponent
functions.description: Disables a built-in k3s component (`--disable=<component>`).

parameters.name: component
parameters.description: The component name to disable. Common values include `traefik`, `servicelb`, `metrics-server`, `coredns`, and `local-storage`. Call this method multiple times to disable more than one component.

functions.name: withExtraArg
functions.description: Appends a raw argument to the `k3s server` command line.
functions.remarks:
Use `WithDisabledComponent` or the dedicated CIDR methods when possible.
This method is intended for flags that have no dedicated helper.

parameters.name: arg
parameters.description: The raw argument to append, e.g. `--write-kubeconfig-mode=644`. Call this method multiple times to append additional arguments.

functions.name: withHelmImage
functions.description: Overrides the container image used to run `helm upgrade --install` for all `HelmReleaseResource` children of this cluster.
functions.signature: withHelmImage(tag?: string, image?: string, registry?: string): K3sClusterResource

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Image tag, e.g. `3.18.0`. `null` keeps the current value.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: Image name, e.g. `alpine/helm`. `null` keeps the current value.

parameters.name: registry
parameters.description: Registry, e.g. `docker.io`. `null` keeps the current value.

functions.name: withHelmValue
functions.description: Adds a Helm `--set key=value` override to the release.
functions.remarks:
Helm `--set` metacharacters (`,`, `{`, `}`, `\`) in
`key` or `value` are automatically escaped.
For values that contain these characters in ways Helm cannot represent safely,
use `WithHelmValuesFile` instead.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The Helm value path using dot notation, e.g. `server.service.type`. If the same key is set more than once, the last call wins.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to assign.

functions.name: withHelmValuesFile
functions.description: Supplies a YAML values file to the Helm release (`--values`).
functions.remarks:
Use this method for structured overrides — particularly values containing commas,
braces, or backslashes that cannot be safely expressed with `WithHelmValue`.
Values files are applied before `--set` flags, so `WithHelmValue`
always takes precedence.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path to the YAML values file on the host. Relative paths are resolved against the AppHost project directory. Call this method multiple times to supply additional files; they are applied in declaration order and later files win for duplicate keys.

functions.name: withK3sVersion
functions.description: Sets the k3s image version used by the cluster server and all its agent nodes.
functions.remarks:
All agent nodes are immediately synced to the same tag to prevent version skew
beyond the Kubernetes-supported ±1 minor version limit. The image tag is part of
the DCP container identity, so synchronisation must happen at configuration time.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The k3s container image tag, e.g. `v1.32.3-k3s1`. Must follow the `v{major}.{minor}.{patch}-k3s{n}` format.

functions.name: withKubectlImage
functions.description: Overrides the container image used to run `kubectl apply` for all `K8sManifestResource` children of this cluster.
functions.signature: withKubectlImage(tag?: string, image?: string, registry?: string): K3sClusterResource

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Image tag, e.g. `1.37.0`. `null` keeps the current value.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: Image name, e.g. `alpine/kubectl`. `null` keeps the current value.

parameters.name: registry
parameters.description: Registry, e.g. `docker.io`. `null` keeps the current value.

functions.name: withLifetime
functions.description: Sets the container lifetime for the k3s cluster and all its agent nodes.
functions.remarks:
Agent nodes are propagated immediately because DCP uses
`ContainerLifetimeAnnotation` to compute container identity. Deferring
propagation to `BeforeStartEvent` would be too late — DCP determines whether
to reuse or recreate a persistent container before that event fires, so agents
would lose their persistent identity and be recreated as new containers each run.

parameters.name: lifetime
parameters.description: The container lifetime to apply.

functions.name: withPodSubnet
functions.description: Sets the CIDR range for pod IP addresses (`--cluster-cidr`).

parameters.name: cidr
parameters.description: The pod subnet in CIDR notation, e.g. `10.42.0.0/16`.

functions.name: withServiceSubnet
functions.description: Sets the CIDR range for Service cluster IPs (`--service-cidr`).

parameters.name: cidr
parameters.description: The service subnet in CIDR notation, e.g. `10.43.0.0/16`.

handleTypes.name: HelmReleaseResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a Helm chart release installed into a k3s cluster.
handleTypes.remarks:
The release runs as an `alpine/helm` container. The container polls until the cluster
kubeconfig is available, executes `helm upgrade --install --wait`, then exits with
code 0. Use `WaitForCompletion(helmRelease)` on resources that depend on the chart
being fully installed.

capabilities.name: namespace
capabilities.description: Gets the Kubernetes namespace the chart is installed into.

capabilities.name: parent
capabilities.description: Gets the Parent property

capabilities.name: releaseName
capabilities.description: Gets the Helm release name passed to `helm upgrade --install`.

capabilities.name: withHelmValue
capabilities.description: Adds a Helm `--set key=value` override to the release.
capabilities.remarks:
Helm `--set` metacharacters (`,`, `{`, `}`, `\`) in
`key` or `value` are automatically escaped.
For values that contain these characters in ways Helm cannot represent safely,
use `WithHelmValuesFile` instead.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The Helm value path using dot notation, e.g. `server.service.type`. If the same key is set more than once, the last call wins.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to assign.

capabilities.name: withHelmValuesFile
capabilities.description: Supplies a YAML values file to the Helm release (`--values`).
capabilities.remarks:
Use this method for structured overrides — particularly values containing commas,
braces, or backslashes that cannot be safely expressed with `WithHelmValue`.
Values files are applied before `--set` flags, so `WithHelmValue`
always takes precedence.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path to the YAML values file on the host. Relative paths are resolved against the AppHost project directory. Call this method multiple times to supply additional files; they are applied in declaration order and later files win for duplicate keys.

handleTypes.name: K3sClusterResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a k3s Kubernetes cluster running as a privileged container resource. Injects the cluster's kubeconfig into a dependent resource. - -

capabilities.name: addHelmRelease
capabilities.description: Installs a Helm chart into the k3s cluster.
capabilities.remarks:
The release runs as an `alpine/helm` container on the DCP network. No host-side
`helm` binary is required. The container exits with code 0 when the release is
fully installed and all workloads are ready. Use `WaitForCompletion(helmRelease)`
on resources that must start only after the chart is ready.
Customize the release with `WithHelmValue` for individual key/value pairs
or `WithHelmValuesFile` to supply a full YAML values file.
capabilities.signature: addHelmRelease(name: string, chart: string, repo?: string, version?: string, namespace?: string): HelmReleaseResource

parameters.description: The Aspire resource name; also used as the Helm release name passed to `helm upgrade --install`.

parameters.name: chart

parameters.name: repo

parameters.name: version

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: The Kubernetes namespace to install the release into. The namespace is created automatically if it does not exist. Defaults to `default`.

handleTypes.name: K3sServiceEndpointResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a Kubernetes Service exposed from a k3s cluster as an Aspire endpoint resource.
handleTypes.remarks:
An in-process WebSocket port-forward bridges the cluster service to a host port when the
cluster is ready. Dependent resources receive the service URL via the standard Aspire
service-discovery environment variable `services__{name}__url`:
-
-
Use `WaitFor(endpoint)` on dependent resources to ensure the port-forward is active
before they start.

capabilities.name: connectionStringEnvironmentVariable
capabilities.description: The environment variable name used to inject the service URL into dependents (`services__{name}__url`), following the Aspire service-discovery convention.

capabilities.name: connectionStringExpression
capabilities.description: Gets the manifest expression for the service URL. Resolves to `http(s)://localhost:{hostPort}` when the endpoint is ready.

capabilities.name: hostPort
capabilities.description: Gets the host port bound by the port-forward listener.
capabilities.remarks:
Zero until the resource transitions to `Running`. Use `WaitFor(endpoint)`
to ensure this is populated before reading it from a dependent resource.

capabilities.name: namespace
capabilities.description: Gets the Kubernetes namespace that contains the Service.

capabilities.name: parent
capabilities.description: Gets the Parent property

capabilities.name: serviceName
capabilities.description: Gets the name of the Kubernetes Service being forwarded.

capabilities.name: servicePort
capabilities.description: Gets the port number declared on the Kubernetes Service.

capabilities.name: setHostPort
capabilities.description: Gets the host port bound by the port-forward listener.
capabilities.remarks:
Zero until the resource transitions to `Running`. Use `WaitFor(endpoint)`
to ensure this is populated before reading it from a dependent resource.

handleTypes.name: K8sManifestResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a Kubernetes manifest (or Kustomize overlay) applied to the parent k3s cluster.
handleTypes.remarks:
The resource runs as an `alpine/kubectl` container. It polls until the cluster
kubeconfig is available, then applies the manifests with `kubectl apply --server-side`
and waits for any CRDs to reach the `Established` condition before exiting with code 0.
Use `WaitForCompletion(manifest)` on resources that depend on these manifests being applied.

capabilities.name: parent
capabilities.description: Gets the Parent property

capabilities.name: path
capabilities.description: Gets the absolute host path to the YAML file, plain directory, or Kustomize directory that contains the manifests to apply.
